disclaimer: Prototype disclaimer: This screen reflects a manual March 20, 2026 market snapshot, but it is not validated for trading, investment, or risk decisions. It should be connected to real-time market and news sources and monitored before production use.

topThemes.impact: Oil, inflation expectations, global risk appetite

rateCurveRead: As of the March 20 snapshot, front-end and long-end yields remained elevated as markets reassessed inflation and policy risk in light of the latest oil shock.

crossMarket.driver: Cyclicals pressured by oil and yields

drivers.name: Middle East escalation
drivers.detail: Iran’s attack on a Kuwaiti refinery and broader war fears pushed energy and inflation risks back to the center of the tape.
drivers.linked: Oil, inflation expectations, global risk appetite

drivers.name: Higher-for-longer repricing
drivers.detail: Investors are dialing down rate-cut expectations as elevated energy prices threaten to keep inflation hotter for longer.

drivers.name: Growth-stock pressure
drivers.detail: Higher yields and persistent macro uncertainty continue to weigh more heavily on duration-sensitive technology names.

drivers.name: Energy leadership
drivers.detail: Energy remains one of the few areas with relative strength as crude stays elevated despite volatile headlines.

risks.title: Inflation shock persistence
risks.desc: High oil and gas prices risk feeding through to inflation and delaying any policy relief.

risks.title: Renewed equity valuation compression
risks.desc: Rising yields and falling cut expectations continue to pressure equity multiples, especially in growth.

risks.title: Cross-asset volatility spike
risks.desc: War headlines are driving abrupt moves across oil, bonds, equities, and currencies.

risks.title: Weekend gap risk
risks.desc: Escalation risk into the weekend can leave markets vulnerable to sharp reopening moves.

timeline.headline: Iran struck a Kuwaiti refinery, intensifying energy-supply fears.
timeline.effect: Oil moved higher and inflation concerns re-accelerated.

timeline.headline: U.S. stocks opened lower as investors reassessed rate-cut hopes.
timeline.effect: Risk appetite weakened and bond yields stayed elevated.

timeline.headline: Nasdaq underperformed while energy stocks held relative strength.
timeline.effect: Growth pressure deepened as macro leadership narrowed.

timeline.headline: Markets remained fragile into the weekend with oil near $108.

strategies.title: Stay Defensive
strategies.body: With oil high, yields elevated, and geopolitical risk still climbing, defensive positioning remains favored until markets show cleaner stabilization.
strategies.trigger: Oil holds high and yields remain elevated

strategies.title: Fade Growth Rallies
strategies.body: Any bounce in growth-heavy equities remains vulnerable while the macro backdrop keeps tightening financial conditions.
strategies.trigger: Nasdaq rallies but yields and oil stay firm

strategies.title: Energy Leadership Bias
strategies.body: Energy can continue to outperform on a relative basis as long as supply fears remain unresolved and crude stays elevated.

strategies.title: Watch Weekend Risk
strategies.body: Geopolitical escalation into the weekend raises the odds of sharp gap moves when markets reopen.
